title: Preparation of Competent Cells (10β E. coli Strain)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fvr7f","text":"2023 NUS-Singapore iGEM team followed this protocol to make competent cells that would be used for transformation.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"acrud","text":"This protocol demonstrates the process of making 5 tubes of competent cells from a   cell culture. Generally, within our protocol, every   of cultured cells can be transformed into 5 tubes of competent cells, with each tube containing   of cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":83,"length":1},{"key":1,"offset":137,"length":1},{"key":2,"offset":235,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"20vmb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"5","error":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":2}},"1":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"5","error":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":2}},"2":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"50","error":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":1}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"dbc0q","text":"Proper lab PPE must be worn at all times.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4vo06","text":"Thermal gloves shall be worn when handling cell stock from the -80°C fridge.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare a Falcon tube with 5 mL of LB media.
Prepare an ice box.
Take out a tube of Sample cell stock from the -80 °C fridge and put it into the ice box.
Prepare a new Falcon tube and add 10 mL of LB media into the tube.
Add 100 µL of the pre-cultured cells into this new Falcon tube to refresh the cells.
Incubate the cells at 37 °C for 02:00:00 .
Prepare a 1 mL mixed solution composed of 20% glycerol and 80% 0.1M CaCl 2 solution, and put it into the ice box to cool its temperature down.
Incubate the cells in an incubator at 37 °C for Overnight
In the biosafety cabinet (BSC), use an inoculation loop to inoculate some competent cells into the Falcon tube with 5 mL of LB media.
Pre-cool the centrifuge machine to 4 °C .
Take out the Falcon tube from the incubator, ensuring that the optical density (OD) of the cultured cells is 0.6OD to 0.8OD.
Place the Falcon tube in ice for 00:30:00 .
Prepare 10 mL of 0.1M MgCl 2 and 10 mL of 0.1M CaCl 2, put both tubes in ice for 00:30:00 .

Centrifuge the Falcon tube with cultured cells in the pre-cooled centrifuge machine at 5000 rpm for 00:05:00 . The temperature in the centrifuge machine must be kept at 4 °C the whole time in the "Cell Washing" section.
Discard the supernatant and keep the cell pellet.
Add a small amount of MgCl 2 solution prepared in the earlier step into the Falcon tube to resuspend the cell pellet. Then, pour the rest of the MgCl 2 solution into the Falcon tube.
Centrifuge the Falcon tube again at 5000 rpm for 00:05:00 .
Discard the supernatant and keep the cell pellet.
Place the Falcon tube in ice for 01:00:00 .
Centrifuge the Falcon tube with the cells at 5000 rpm for 00:05:00 .
Discard the supernatant and keep the cell pellet.
Add a small amount of CaCl 2 solution prepared in the earlier step into the Falcon tube to resuspend the cell pellet. Then, pour the rest of the CaCl 2 solution into the Falcon tube.
Add 250 µL of glycerol-CaCl 2 solution into the Falcon tube with the cell pellet and resuspend the cell pellet.
Split the cells in the Falcon tube into 5 new Eppendorf tubes with each tube containing 50 µL of cells.
The competent cells can be stored in a -80°C fridge or used immediately.
creator: NUS iGEM
